channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Police rescue 15 people from Tampa home after Milton knocks tree on house
item.description: Officers with the Tampa Police Department were sheltering a few miles when they were alerted to the incident via a 911 call.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CNN's Anderson Cooper hit by flying object during Milton live shot
item.description: CNN’s Anderson Cooper was hit by a flying object on Wednesday during a live shot while reporting from Florida on Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Milton and Helene leave students, schools reeling
item.description: As Hurricane Milton slams into Florida — and amid Helene's lingering destruction — students across the southeast are facing severe disruptions to their academic year, posing challenges for both educators and children after they have already witnessed devastation in their communities.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Arizona college student accused in random classmate stabbing has attempted murder charge dropped
item.description: ASU stabbing suspect Kaci Sloan has entered a not-guilty plea. Sloan is charged with stabbing a fellow student multiple times in a classroom on Sept. 19.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Hurricane Milton weakens to Category 1, but danger remains in Florida
item.description: Hurricane Milton is bringing powerful winds, a storm surge and flooding to much of Florida after making landfall along the Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 5 takeaways from the Arizona Senate debate
item.description: Rep. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) and Republican candidate Kari Lake faced off in their only debate in the Arizona Senate race Wednesday, and the two contenders frequently traded barbs. Gallego and Lake, who are vying for retiring Sen.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Milton spawns destructive, deadly tornadoes before making landfall
item.description: Multiple powerful tornadoes ripped across Florida hours before Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday, tearing off roofs, overturning vehicles and sucking debris into the air as the black V-shaped columns moved through. Deaths were reported in St.
item.dc:creator: SUMAN NAISHADHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Video: Hurricane Milton sucks water out of Tampa Bay
item.description: Tampa Bay experienced a rare reverse storm surge as Hurricane Milton made landfall Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taylor Swift donates $5 million to Hurricane Helene and Milton relief efforts
item.description: Taylor Swift donated $5 million to Feeding America’s disaster relief efforts for those affected by ­­Hurricane Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pat Ryan, Alison Esposito face off in NY-18 debate on PIX11
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Democratic U.S. Rep. Pat Ryan and Republican Alison Esposito, the two candidates running to represent New York's 18th Congressional District, faced off in a debate on PIX11 Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's 'God Bless the USA' Bibles printed in China, records show
item.description: Thousands of copies of former President Trump's "God Bless the USA" Bible were printed in China, according to trade records.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump-Putin ties are back in the spotlight after new book describes calls
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A new book's assertion that former President Donald Trump may have had as many as seven private phone calls with Russian President Vladimir Putin since leaving the White House has refocused attention on their politically fraught relationship and on Trump's sustained dialogue with w
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tampa's 'Lt. Dan' says he's riding out Hurricane Milton on boat despite mayor's warning
item.description: A Tampa man who goes by the nickname "Lt. Dan" has caused a flurry of online concern for his safety during Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NASA video shows Hurricane Milton from space
item.description: Video taken from the International Space Station puts the massive size of Hurricane Milton into perspective.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: In remote mountain communities cut off by Helene, residents look to the skies for aid
item.description: National Guard members who deployed to western North Carolina are still carrying out aid missions nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Climate change boosted Helene's deadly rain and wind and the same is likely for Milton: Scientists
item.description: Human-caused climate change boosted a devastating Hurricane Helene 's rainfall by about 10% and intensified its winds by about 11%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Over 9 million pounds of ready-to-eat meat, poultry recalled: USDA
item.description: The products may be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes, according to the U.S. Department of Agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Michael Reiner, ABC 4

item.title: Honda recalls about 1.7 million vehicles over steering issue
item.description: Honda is recalling around 1.7 million vehicles in the U.S. over a steering issue that could result in a crash, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: How Waffle House helps Southerners — and FEMA — judge a storm's severity
item.description: Golden hashbrowns, gravy-smothered biscuits and crispy waffles with a hearty helping of maple syrup are among the classic Southern comfort foods. But when hurricanes tear through Southeastern towns, the hot meals and bold yellow signs of the local Waffle House provide another kind of comfort.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: iPhone update allows users to text for help when there's no cell service. How it works
item.description: With Hurricane Milton barreling toward Florida's Gulf Coast, many Tampa Bay area residents are fearing widespread and potentially weeks-long power outages, which could affect cellphone service, as many saw during Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, ABC 4

item.title: Georgia university leaders ask NCAA to ban transgender women from sports
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The regents who govern Georgia's 26 public universities and colleges voted on Tuesday to ask the NCAA and another college athletic federation to ban transgender women from participating in women's sports. The unanimous vote came after Georgia Lt. Gov.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A retired Houston officer gets 60 years in a couple's drug raid deaths that revealed corruption
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A former Houston police officer was sentenced to 60 years in prison on Tuesday for the murder of a married couple during a drug raid that revealed systemic corruption in the department’s narcotics unit and prompted criticism of the law enforcement agency and its tactics.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How will Hurricane Milton stack up against other major recent storms?
item.description: As a ferocious Hurricane Milton charges its way across the Gulf Coast with Tampa and St. Petersburg in its sights, thousands flee the area for safer ground, leaving behind debris from Hurricane Helene's destruction less that two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fentanyl hidden within carne asada beef seized in Fresno County
item.description: A Washington man has been arrested after a canine officer uncovered $500,000 worth of fentanyl hidden in carne asada beef packages and a tote cooler, the California Highway Patrol said.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Meza, Fox 5

item.title: Muggers ripped watch off Dodgers pitcher Walker Buehler's arm, police say
item.description:
Police in California say thieves surrounded Los Angeles Dodgers starting pitcher Walker Buehler last month and ripped a pricey watch off his arm.
Police say the victims were all targets of organized groups who steal high-end watches in large crowds during events.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump campaign blasts Bob Woodward; denies secret Putin calls
item.description: Donald Trump's campaign is rejecting claims in Bob Woodward's new book 'War' about the former president holding secret calls with Vladimir Putin since leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Equestrian found dead in Montana river after missing for days, horse and cellphone left behind
item.description: Meghan Rita Rouns, 27, was last seen alive on Friday before she went riding in near Bozeman, Montana. Although her unaccompanied horse and phone have been recovered, she has not.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canyoneer dies after accidental 150-foot fall at Zion National Park
item.description: A man fell while canyoneering at Zion National Park on Saturday, resulting in his death, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, Fox 5

item.title: Why is Tampa Bay so vulnerable to hurricanes?
item.description: As Milton barrels toward the Gulf Coast of Florida, experts are worried that a century of luck could come to a sudden end.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What polling shows about Black voters' views of Harris and Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Black registered voters have an overwhelmingly positive view of Vice President Kamala Harris, but they’re less sure that she would change the country for the better, according to a recent poll from the AP-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The mayor of a state capital in Mexico is killed less than 1 week after he took office
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Relatives on Monday buried the mayor of a state capital in southern Mexico who was killed just one week after he took office. Photos of the crime scene shared on social media showed Mayor Alejandro Arcos’ severed head had been left on the roof of a pickup truck.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Federal prosecutors quietly dismiss Chasing Horse's long-dormant sex abuse case
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Federal prosecutors in Nevada have quietly dismissed a long-dormant sex abuse case against Nathan Chasing Horse, though the former “Dances with Wolves" actor still faces criminal charges elsewhere. The federal case was tossed Oct.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tossed balls from stands, apparently aimed at Profar, interrupt Padres' win in NLDS Game 2
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Tempers flared on the field and in the stands at Dodger Stadium, with rowdy fans tossing baseballs, apparently at San Diego left fielder Jurickson Profar, and then trash that caused a 12-minute wait between pitches during the seventh inning of the Padres' 10-2 win in NL Division S
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5
